title: Golden Master Corpus testing for CLI behavior validation during language ports
context: Porting an application to a new language requires verifying exact output parity across extensive CLI surfaces.
content: Use a pre-recorded golden master corpus to assert that the ported CLI produces byte-identical stdout, stderr, and output artifacts. Normalize volatile fields (such as timestamps, system paths, and git SHAs) within the test harness before comparison to avoid false failures, while ensuring true behavioral divergence is cleanly flagged.
anti_patterns: Writing separate manual tests for each ported CLI command instead of relying on a generalized golden corpus
Failing to normalize volatile outputs, leading to flaky test runs due to dynamic execution environment changes
